_metadata.title: CMS CPT/HCPCS Modifiers (Common Subset)
_metadata.source: CMS Medicare Claims Processing Manual + AMA CPT modifiers; ANSI HCPCS Level II modifiers
_metadata.description: Two-character modifiers appended to CPT/HCPCS procedure codes. Used by validate_modifier and modifier-CPT compatibility checks. Lists describe common, commercially-significant modifiers; exotic modifiers are intentionally omitted to keep the validator focused.
_metadata.schema: code: { description, category, applies_to_categories[], blocks_with[], requires_documentation }
_metadata.applies_to_categories: CPT category ranges (E_M, surgery, radiology, pathology, medicine, anesthesia, hcpcs_level_ii) where the modifier is conventionally valid.
_metadata.blocks_with: Modifiers that are mutually exclusive with this one on the same line.
_metadata.requires_documentation: Whether the modifier typically requires supporting documentation.

22.description: Increased Procedural Services - When the work required to provide a service is substantially greater than typically required.

23.description: Unusual Anesthesia - When a procedure normally not requiring anesthesia must be done under anesthesia.

24.description: Unrelated E/M Service by Same Physician During a Postoperative Period.

25.description: Significant, Separately Identifiable E/M Service by Same Physician on the Same Day of a Procedure.

26.description: Professional Component - Used to bill the professional (physician) interpretation of a procedure (e.g. radiology read).

27.description: Multiple Outpatient Hospital E/M Encounters on the Same Date.

32.description: Mandated Services (e.g. third-party payer, governmental, legislative or regulatory requirement).

33.description: Preventive Services - Indicates a service that is preventive in nature.

47.description: Anesthesia by Surgeon - Regional or general anesthesia provided by the surgeon.

50.description: Bilateral Procedure - A procedure performed on both sides of the body during the same session.

51.description: Multiple Procedures - When multiple procedures, other than E/M services, are performed at the same session by the same provider.

52.description: Reduced Services - When a service or procedure is partially reduced or eliminated at the physician's election.

53.description: Discontinued Procedure - When the physician elects to terminate a surgical or diagnostic procedure due to extenuating circumstances.

54.description: Surgical Care Only - One physician performs the surgical procedure but another provides preoperative and/or postoperative care.

55.description: Postoperative Management Only - One physician performs only the postoperative management.

56.description: Preoperative Management Only - One physician performs only the preoperative management.

57.description: Decision for Surgery - An E/M service that resulted in the initial decision to perform a major surgery.

58.description: Staged or Related Procedure or Service by the Same Physician During the Postoperative Period.

59.description: Distinct Procedural Service - Used to identify procedures/services not normally reported together but appropriate under the circumstances.

62.description: Two Surgeons - When two surgeons work together as primary surgeons performing distinct parts of a single procedure.

63.description: Procedure Performed on Infants Less than 4 kg.

66.description: Surgical Team - For complex procedures requiring concomitant services of several physicians.

76.description: Repeat Procedure or Service by Same Physician.

78.description: Unplanned Return to the Operating/Procedure Room by the Same Physician Following Initial Procedure for a Related Procedure During the Postoperative Period.

79.description: Unrelated Procedure or Service by the Same Physician During the Postoperative Period.

81.description: Minimum Assistant Surgeon - Minimum surgical assistant services.

82.description: Assistant Surgeon (when qualified resident surgeon not available).

90.description: Reference (Outside) Laboratory - A laboratory procedure performed by a party other than the treating or reporting physician.

91.description: Repeat Clinical Diagnostic Laboratory Test - When subsequent reruns to obtain multiple test results on the same patient on the same day are necessary.

95.description: Synchronous Telemedicine Service via Real-Time Interactive Audio and Video Telecommunications System.

99.description: Multiple Modifiers - When more than two modifiers are needed.

AS.description: Physician Assistant, Nurse Practitioner, or Clinical Nurse Specialist Services for Assistant at Surgery.

GA.description: Waiver of Liability Statement Issued as Required by Payer Policy, Individual Case.

GT.description: Via Interactive Audio and Video Telecommunication Systems (legacy telehealth modifier).

GZ.description: Item or Service Expected to Be Denied as Not Reasonable and Necessary.

LT.description: Left Side - Used to identify procedures performed on the left side of the body.

RT.description: Right Side - Used to identify procedures performed on the right side of the body.

TC.description: Technical Component - Used to bill the technical (equipment, supplies, technician) portion of a procedure.

XE.description: Separate Encounter - A service that is distinct because it occurred during a separate encounter.

XS.description: Separate Structure - A service that is distinct because it was performed on a separate organ/structure.

XP.description: Separate Practitioner - A service that is distinct because it was performed by a different practitioner.

XU.description: Unusual Non-Overlapping Service - The use of a service that is distinct because it does not overlap usual components of the main service.
